title: Network Protocol (NetP) v1
description: Standard protocol for describing social network datasets. A filled instance of this schema serves as a complete description of an empirical network suitable for inclusion in an academic article or a public repository.

schema_version.description: Version of the NetP schema used to validate this instance.

dataset_doi.description: Unique identifier for the dataset (e.g. a DOI). Optional; may be assigned upon registration in a repository.

nodes.description: One entry for one-mode networks; two entries for two-mode networks (one per mode).

ties.description: One entry per relationship. Univariate networks have one; multivariate have as many as the network has distinct relationships.

additional_information.description: Free-text field for any information not captured by the structured fields above. Not intended for article-specific results or analysis details.

allOf.description: Cross-mode consistency: if mode is two-mode, the nodes array must have two entries.
If multivariate, ties must have at least two entries.

description.description: One-line, concise, specific description of the network. Should capture the kind of nodes, the kind of ties, the empirical context, and the time frame.

temporal.description: Cross-sectional networks describe a single time frame. Longitudinal networks describe multiple time frames among the same set of actors.

variability.description: Univariate networks include only one type of relationship. Multivariate networks include multiple types of relationships between the same entities.

mode.description: One-mode networks have ties between entities of a single type. Two-mode networks have ties between entities of two distinct types (e.g. people and events).

number_of_waves.description: Distinct instances when network data among the same set of actors was collected. Required for longitudinal data; omit for cross-sectional.

number_of_relationships.description: Number of distinct types of relationships represented in the data. Required for multivariate data; omit for univariate.

initial_date.description: Initial date of the data. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, YYYY-MM-DD, or the literal string 'unknown' if not reported in the source.

final_date.description: Final date of the data. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, YYYY-MM-DD, or the literal string 'unknown' if not reported in the source.

wave_dates.description: For longitudinal data: date of each wave. The number of entries should equal overview.number_of_waves.

associated_publication.description: DOI or URL of the primary publication associated with the data, if any.

source_of_data.description: Where the information comes from. Suggested values include 'archival', 'survey', 'observation', 'interview', 'database', 'digital trace', 'experimental'. Authors are encouraged to be as specific as possible.

details.description: Any information relevant about the source of the data: name of the survey or archive, the larger project the data belongs to, specifics about the sample or its context, etc.

method.description: How the data was collected (for non-archival sources): e.g. online questionnaire, pen-and-paper questionnaire, phone interview, in-person interview, direct observation, automated logging.

general_typology.description: General category of nodes. Suggested values: 'individuals', 'groups of people', 'organizations', 'animals', 'objects', 'plants', 'places', 'words', 'concepts', 'events'. Authors may use their own category if needed.

network_boundary.description: Criteria used to delimit which nodes are included in the network.

node_exclusion.description: Criteria used to exclude nodes that fell within the boundary (e.g. removing isolates, outliers, non-respondents).

node_transformation.description: Manipulations performed on the nodes to obtain their final shape (e.g. aggregating multiple entities into a single node).

label.description: Short identifier for the relationship, used to link this tie set to its statistics entries (e.g. 'marriage', 'advice', 'communication').

general_typology.description: Top-level category of the relationship, following Borgatti et al. (2009). Subcategories like 'kinship' or 'affective' belong in 'detailed_typology' rather than here — they sit under 'social relations'. Subcategories like 'membership' or 'attribute' sit under 'similarities'.

detailed_typology.description: Specific category of the relationship, e.g. 'friendship', 'advice', 'marriage (kinship)', 'co-membership', 'email exchange'. When the original article uses a Borgatti subcategory label (e.g. 'affective', 'kinship', 'membership'), the convention is to record the specific relationship followed by the subcategory in parentheses.

self_links.description: Whether nodes can have ties to themselves.

represents.description: If weighted: what the weight represents (e.g. frequency of communication, amount of trade).

range.description: If weighted: range of values, e.g. '0-100' or '1-7'.

unit.description: If weighted: unit of measurement, e.g. 'thousands of euros', 'messages per day'.

transformation.description: Manipulations performed on the ties (e.g. dichotomization, symmetrization, aggregation across reports).

maximum_outdegree.description: Limit placed during data collection on the number of outgoing ties per entity, if any. Provide either an integer or a brief description.

name_generator.description: Exact phrase used to elicit a list of alters (for survey/interview data).

type_of_name_generator.description: Whether participants were presented with a roster of names or asked to recall names freely (for survey/interview data).

name_interpreter.description: Exact phrase used to obtain additional information about each tie (for survey/interview data).

relationship.description: Label of the relationship this statistics entry describes. Must match the 'label' of an entry in the top-level 'ties' array.

wave.description: Wave index, starting at 1. Cross-sectional networks always use 1.

wave_date.description: Date of this wave, if longitudinal. Accepts YYYY, YYYY-MM, or YYYY-MM-DD.

network_size.description: Number of nodes in the final network. One entry for one-mode networks; two entries (one per mode) for two-mode networks.

response_rate.description: Survey response rate. Expressed as a proportion in [0, 1].

number_of_ties.description: Total number of edges (undirected) or arcs (directed) in the final network.

density.description: Number of ties divided by the number of potential ties.

average_path_length.description: Mean geodesic distance between any two reachable nodes.

average_degree.description: Number of ties divided by the number of nodes. One entry for one-mode networks; two entries (one per mode) for two-mode networks.

number_of_isolates.description: Number of nodes with zero indegree and zero outdegree.

clustering_coefficient.description: Average interconnectedness of each node's neighbors. See Watts (1999).

degree_centralization.description: Extent to which the degree distribution is skewed. Use for undirected networks.

reciprocity.description: Proportion of arcs for which the reverse arc also exists. Use for directed networks.

indegree_centralization.description: Indegree-based degree centralization. Use for directed networks.

outdegree_centralization.description: Outdegree-based degree centralization. Use for directed networks.

number_of_new_ties.description: Number of ties present at this wave but absent at the previous one. Use for longitudinal networks (wave > 1).

number_of_lost_ties.description: Number of ties present at the previous wave but absent at this one. Use for longitudinal networks (wave > 1).
